argument_substitution.mechanism: Cursor's custom command documentation does not describe a dedicated argument-placeholder syntax; command bodies are plain Markdown.

builtin_commands.mechanism: Cursor ships built-in slash commands in the IDE; user-authored commands live alongside them and do not override the built-in set.

providerExtensions.name: Filename as command name
providerExtensions.summary: Each .md file under .cursor/commands/ becomes a slash command named after the file (introduced in Cursor 1.6+); there is no explicit name frontmatter field.

providerExtensions.name: Plain Markdown body
providerExtensions.summary: Custom commands are plain Markdown files; the body becomes the prompt text sent when the command is invoked, with no required frontmatter.

providerExtensions.name: Project and global command scopes
providerExtensions.summary: Commands live in .cursor/commands/ at the project root for project scope and in ~/.cursor/commands/ for user-global scope.
